Molecular Pathology / Next-Generation Sequencing Laboratory

                     (triple-negative), Stage IIB (T2 N1 M0, AJCC 8th ed.)

                     left breast upper outer quadrant (primary tumor)

Assay:               Comprehensive Genomic Profiling (CGP), tissue-based, DNA + RNA
                     324-gene targeted panel; OncoPanel v4 (illustrative)
Sequencing Platform: Illumina NovaSeq 6000
Mean Coverage:       650x (tumor); 350x (matched normal not submitted; germline

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
QUALITY METRICS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
QC Status:           PASS
Total Reads:         ~48 million (deduplicated)
Uniformity (% bases >= 0.2x mean):  97.4%
Insert Size (median):  180 bp
Exon Coverage >= 100x: 98.6%
Sample Adequacy:     Adequate (>20% tumor nuclei, sufficient DNA yield: 120 ng)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
METHODOLOGY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
DNA was extracted from FFPE-embedded tumor tissue following manual macrodissection to enrich for tumor cellularity. A hybrid-capture approach targeting all coding exons of 324 cancer-relevant genes was performed, encompassing oncogenes, tumor suppressor genes, and genes of clinical actionability in solid tumors. The panel detects single-nucleotide variants (SNVs), small insertions/deletions (indels), copy number alterations (amplifications and homozygous deletions), and gene rearrangements/fusions. Tumor mutational burden (TMB) and microsatellite instability (MSI) are calculated genome-wide using an in silico normalization method. Homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) scoring incorporates genomic loss of heterozygosity (gLOH), large-scale state transitions (LST), and telomeric allelic imbalance (TAI). RNA-based fusion detection was performed from the same FFPE block using an anchored multiplex PCR approach targeting 55 known fusion gene partners. PD-L1 CPS was performed by immunohistochemistry (Dako 22C3 pharmDx assay) on an adjacent section and reported independently; result incorporated herein. A note has been appended regarding reflexed germline testing.
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
GENOMIC FINDINGS -- SUMMARY TABLE

TIER I -- CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT ALTERATIONS WITH DIRECT THERAPEUTIC RELEVANCE

  Gene       Alteration                   Type           VAF     Tier    Notes
  ─────────  ──────────────────────────── ──────────     ──────  ──────  ──────────────────────────────────────
  BRCA1      p.E1250* (c.3750G>A)         Nonsense       51%     IA      Pathogenic; germline-confirmed (see
                                                                          germline note); somatic LOH at BRCA1
                                                                          locus consistent with biallelic loss;
                                                                          therapeutic implication: PARP inhibitor
                                                                          eligibility (olaparib, talazoparib)
  TP53       p.R248W (c.742C>T)           Missense       48%     IA      Well-characterized gain-of-function
                                                                          hotspot mutation; pathogenic; loss of
                                                                          tumor suppressor function; dominant
                                                                          negative effect on wild-type p53
  PTEN       Homozygous deletion          CNA (del)      --      IB      Loss of PTEN protein expression
                                                                          expected; PI3K/AKT pathway activation;
                                                                          potential future relevance for PI3K
                                                                          pathway inhibitors (alpelisib, AKT inh.)

TIER II -- POTENTIALLY ACTIONABLE OR PROGNOSTICALLY RELEVANT

  Gene       Alteration                   Type           VAF     Tier    Notes
  ─────────  ──────────────────────────── ──────────     ──────  ──────  ──────────────────────────────────────
  RB1        p.Q670* (c.2008C>T)          Nonsense       39%     IIB     Pathogenic; loss of retinoblastoma
                                                                          tumor suppressor function; associated
                                                                          with aggressive TNBC biology and
                                                                          resistance to CDK4/6 inhibition;
                                                                          potential predictive marker for
                                                                          chemotherapy sensitivity
  MYC        Amplification                CNA (amp)      --      IIB     Copy number ~14; high-level
                                                                          amplification (> 6 copies); associated
                                                                          with aggressive phenotype and high
                                                                          proliferative index; no direct approved
                                                                          targeted therapy; investigational
                                                                          relevance (BET inhibitors, aurora kinase
                                                                          inhibitors in clinical trials)
  CDKN2A     p.P114L (c.341C>T)           Missense       22%     IIC     Variant of uncertain significance in
                                                                          isolation; subclonal (VAF 22%); CDKN2A
                                                                          encodes p16 (CDK4/6 inhibitor) and p14ARF
                                                                          (p53 stabilizer); functional significance
                                                                          unclear in context of intact wild-type
                                                                          allele at this VAF

TIER III -- VARIANTS OF UNKNOWN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS) -- selected

  No additional VUS of current clinical significance identified beyond those listed above.

BIOGENOMIC SIGNATURES & WHOLE-GENOME METRICS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Microsatellite Status (MSI):        Microsatellite stable (MSS)
                                    -- 35 microsatellite loci interrogated; 0/35 unstable
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB):      9.2 mutations/megabase (intermediate)
                                    -- Threshold for TMB-high per FDA: >= 10 mut/Mb
                                    -- Not TMB-high by this assay
Homologous Recombination Deficiency (HRD) Score: 54 (POSITIVE)
                                    -- Threshold for HRD positivity: >= 42
                                    -- Genomic loss of heterozygosity (gLOH): 22%
                                    -- Large-scale state transitions (LST): 18
                                    -- Telomeric allelic imbalance (TAI): 14
                                    -- HRD positivity driven by BRCA1 biallelic loss;
                                       consistent with BRCAness phenotype
Mutational Signatures (SBS, COSMIC v3.3):
                                    -- SBS3 (BRCA1/2 deficiency, HR repair defect): 34%
                                    -- SBS5 (clock-like, aging): 28%
                                    -- SBS13 (APOBEC, C>G at TCA/TCT context): 19%
                                    -- SBS2  (APOBEC, C>T at TCA/TCT context): 14%
                                    -- SBS1  (deamination, CpG): 5%
                                    -- SBS3 dominance consistent with BRCA1-deficient genome
Copy Number Landscape:              Highly disrupted genome; multiple arm-level CNAs
                                    Gains: 1q, 8q (MYC locus), 10p
                                    Losses: 5q, 10q (PTEN locus), 13q (RB1 locus),
                                             17p (TP53 locus), 17q (BRCA1 locus)
                                    Consistent with TNBC / basal-like genomic profile

IMMUNO-ONCOLOGY BIOMARKERS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
PD-L1 CPS (Dako 22C3 pharmDx):     15  (POSITIVE; threshold >= 10)
                                    -- PD-L1 positive tumor cells, tumor-associated
                                       macrophages, and lymphocytes all contributing
                                    -- Supports eligibility for pembrolizumab-based
                                       neoadjuvant and/or adjuvant therapy in TNBC
                                       (KEYNOTE-522 context)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
GERMLINE VARIANT NOTE
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
The BRCA1 p.E1250* (c.3750G>A) variant was identified at a VAF of 51% -- a VAF in the range expected for a heterozygous germline variant in a tumor specimen with significant somatic LOH. The allele frequency, combined with the copy number loss at the BRCA1 locus (17q12-q21), is consistent with biallelic inactivation via germline mutation plus somatic loss of heterozygosity (LOH), a classical two-hit mechanism. This pattern strongly suggests a germline origin. Reflexed germline confirmatory testing was performed from a separate peripheral blood sample. Germline result: PATHOGENIC GERMLINE VARIANT CONFIRMED -- BRCA1 c.3750G>A (p.E1250*, NM_007294.4), heterozygous, classified as Pathogenic (ClinVar ID: associated). A separate, complete germline report has been issued and communicated directly to the ordering provider and genetic counseling service.

IMPLICATIONS: Patient is at significantly elevated lifetime risk for breast cancer (contralateral) and ovarian cancer. Cascade testing of first-degree relatives strongly recommended. PARP inhibitor therapy (e.g., olaparib, talazoparib) may be applicable per current guidelines for germline BRCA1/2-mutated breast cancer.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
INTERPRETATION AND MOLECULAR SUMMARY
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
This triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma (grade 3, stage IIB) harbors a genomic profile consistent with the basal-like / BRCAness molecular subtype, with several clinically significant findings:

1. BIALLELIC BRCA1 INACTIVATION (germline p.E1250* + somatic LOH): This is the most therapeutically relevant finding. BRCA1 loss of function results in profound homologous recombination deficiency (HRD score 54, positive), rendering the tumor sensitive to PARP inhibitors (olaparib, talazoparib) and platinum-based chemotherapy. The germline nature of this variant has critical implications for the patient and her biological relatives.

2. TP53 p.R248W (gain-of-function hotspot): This well-characterized dominant-negative p53 mutation is present at high VAF (48%), consistent with a clonally dominant alteration arising early in tumor evolution. The R248W variant not only abolishes normal p53 transcriptional activity but confers oncogenic gain-of-function properties (enhanced invasion, chemotherapy resistance in some contexts). This alteration is nearly universal in TNBC/basal-like tumors and does not currently have a direct targeted therapeutic option, though ongoing trials with APR-246 (eprenetapopt) and related p53-reactivating agents are in development.

3. PTEN HOMOZYGOUS DELETION: Complete loss of PTEN results in unopposed PI3K/AKT/mTOR pathway activation. In the absence of a PIK3CA-activating mutation (which is absent here, as expected in BRCA1-mutant TNBC), PTEN loss is the primary driver of PI3K pathway dysregulation. This may confer relative resistance to some chemotherapy agents and is a potential future target for PI3K pathway inhibition (alpelisib, capivasertib, ipatasertib).

4. RB1 TRUNCATING MUTATION (p.Q670*): Inactivation of RB1 further destabilizes cell cycle control in a tumor already lacking functional p53. Biologically, combined TP53 and RB1 loss is a hallmark of highly aggressive TNBC and is associated with cisplatin sensitivity in some preclinical models. Notably, RB1 loss renders the tumor intrinsically resistant to CDK4/6 inhibitors (palbociclib, ribociclib, abemaciclib), which is consistent with the ER-negative receptor status and would preclude use of these agents in any future hormone receptor conversion scenario.

5. MYC AMPLIFICATION (copy number ~14): High-level MYC amplification is a recurrent event in TNBC and is associated with high Ki-67 (here 85%) and aggressive clinical behavior. MYC-amplified tumors may be sensitized to Aurora A kinase inhibitors or BET bromodomain inhibitors (investigational). This finding is prognostic but not currently directly actionable with approved therapies.

6. CDKN2A p.P114L (subclonal, VAF 22%): This subclonal variant is of uncertain significance. CDKN2A encodes p16INK4A (a CDK4/6 inhibitor) and p14ARF (a p53 stabilizer). Given pre-existing RB1 inactivation and TP53 mutation, the functional contribution of this CDKN2A variant is likely redundant with respect to cell cycle dysregulation.

7. HRD SIGNATURE: The HRD score of 54, combined with a dominant SBS3 mutational signature (34% of mutations), is entirely consistent with BRCA1-mediated homologous recombination deficiency. This signature validates the functional consequences of BRCA1 biallelic loss at the genomic level. The APOBEC mutational signatures (SBS2/13, combined ~33%) are frequently co-observed in TNBC and may contribute to mutational heterogeneity.

8. PD-L1 CPS 15 (POSITIVE): PD-L1 expression above the threshold of 10 supports eligibility for pembrolizumab in combination with chemotherapy in the early-stage/neoadjuvant and potentially metastatic settings, per KEYNOTE-522 and KEYNOTE-355 data.

MOLECULAR DIAGNOSIS: Triple-negative invasive ductal carcinoma, basal-like genomic subtype, with germline BRCA1 pathogenic variant (biallelic inactivation), TP53 gain-of-function mutation, PTEN homozygous deletion, RB1 truncation, MYC amplification, and positive HRD. PD-L1 CPS 15. MSS. TMB 9.2 mut/Mb (not TMB-high).

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
ACTIONABILITY SUMMARY (for treating physician reference)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Biomarker              Finding                    Therapeutic Class          Evidence Level
─────────────────────  ─────────────────────────  ────────────────────────── ──────────────
BRCA1 (germline)       Pathogenic (biallelic)     PARP inhibitors            FDA-approved (olaparib,
                                                  (olaparib, talazoparib)    talazoparib) -- HER2- mBC
                                                                             with gBRCA1/2
PD-L1 CPS              15 (positive, >= 10)       Anti-PD-1 (pembrolizumab)  FDA-approved --
                                                  + chemotherapy             KEYNOTE-522 (neoadjuvant),
                                                                             KEYNOTE-355 (metastatic)
HRD score              54 (positive)              PARP inhibitors,           Supportive biomarker;
                                                  Platinum chemotherapy      HRD+ supports PARP inh.
                                                                             and platinum sensitivity
PTEN loss              Homozygous deletion        PI3K/AKT inhibitors        Investigational;
                                                  (alpelisib, capivasertib,  no current standard
                                                  ipatasertib)               indication in TNBC
RB1                    Truncating mutation        CDK4/6 inhibitors --       Predictive of RESISTANCE
                                                  RESISTANCE marker          to CDK4/6 inh.; not
                                                                             applicable here (ER-)
MYC                    Amplification (CN ~14)     BET inhibitors, Aurora A   Investigational only
                                                  kinase inh. (investigational)
TMB                    9.2 mut/Mb (not high)      Anti-PD-1/L1 monotherapy   TMB not high; does not
                                                                             support TMB-based IO use
MSI                    MSS                        Pembrolizumab (MSI-H only) MSS; not applicable

Note: This report reflects molecular findings at the time of the primary diagnostic biopsy. Repeat molecular profiling of any future metastatic or recurrent specimens is strongly encouraged, as clonal evolution and acquired resistance mechanisms may alter the actionable genomic landscape.
